brief_document.title: Delegated signal, query, and subscribe surface
brief_document.purpose: Surface signal, query, and subscribe on Engine for API completeness, performing the registry-lookup step that AE owns and delegating the delivery mechanics to AT (signal routing, query dispatch) and AD/AT (event publishing). This fixes the method surface and the seam trait signatures without implementing AT's machinery.
brief_document.task: Implement engine/delegated.rs: signal, query, and subscribe methods on Engine. Define the three seam traits the Engine holds — SignalRouter, QueryService, EventPublisher — with concrete signatures (R4), since these are the cross-cluster integration contract AT/AD implement. Each Engine method resolves the target workflow via the registry (the AE-owned lookup) and then calls into the corresponding seam. This brief provides the method surface, the lookup, the trait definitions, and the seam wiring — it does not implement signal routing, query dispatch, or the event publisher.

requirements.title: signal surface with registry lookup
requirements.spec: WHEN signal(&id, &run, name, payload) is called, THE SYSTEM SHALL resolve the target workflow handle via the registry and SHALL hand the signal to the AT signal-routing seam. IF the workflow is not registered, THEN THE SYSTEM SHALL return EngineError::WorkflowNotFound. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT implement the mailbox routing itself.
acceptance: delegated.rs defines Engine::signal that looks up the handle and, on success, calls the AT signal-routing seam (a SignalRouter trait/handle the Engine holds) with the target pid, name, and payload.
grep of delegated.rs shows no direct mailbox-send / selective-receive routing logic — only the seam call.
A test installs a fake SignalRouter seam, signals a registered workflow, and asserts the seam received the (pid, name, payload); signalling an unknown id returns WorkflowNotFound.

requirements.title: query surface with registry lookup
requirements.spec: WHEN query(&id, &run, name) is called, THE SYSTEM SHALL resolve the target workflow handle via the registry and SHALL hand the query to the AT query-dispatch seam, returning the reply Payload. IF the workflow is not registered, THEN THE SYSTEM SHALL return EngineError::WorkflowNotFound. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT implement the query dispatch itself.
acceptance: delegated.rs defines Engine::query that looks up the handle and calls the AT query-dispatch seam (a QueryService trait/handle) with the target pid and query name, returning the reply Payload.
grep of delegated.rs shows no query-handler invocation logic — only the seam call.
A test installs a fake QueryService seam returning a known Payload and asserts query returns it for a registered workflow.

requirements.title: subscribe surface delegating to the event publisher
requirements.spec: WHEN subscribe(filter: EventFilter) is called, THE SYSTEM SHALL return an event stream produced by the AD/AT event-publishing seam. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT implement the broadcast/publishing mechanism itself.
acceptance: delegated.rs defines Engine::subscribe(filter) -> stream that delegates to the event-publisher seam (an EventPublisher trait/handle the Engine holds) and returns its filtered stream.
grep of delegated.rs shows no broadcast-channel implementation — only the seam call.
A test installs a fake EventPublisher seam that emits two events and asserts subscribe yields them filtered by the given EventFilter.

requirements.title: Concrete seam trait signatures
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL define the SignalRouter, QueryService, and EventPublisher seam traits with concrete signatures, since AT/AD implement them and both sides must agree before independent dispatch.
acceptance: SignalRouter: `async fn route(&self, target: &WorkflowHandle, name: String, payload: Payload) -> Result<(), EngineError>` — records SignalReceived (via the workflow's Recorder) and delivers to the mailbox; AT-005/AT-006 implement it.

QueryService: `async fn query(&self, target: &WorkflowHandle, name: String) -> Result<Payload, EngineError>` — dispatches the read-only query and returns the handler reply (mapping QueryError::Timeout/Unknown/NotRunning into EngineError); AT-007 implements it.

EventPublisher: `fn subscribe(&self, filter: EventFilter) -> BoxStream<'static, Event>` (or an explicit named stream type) — yields the filtered live event stream; AD/AT implement it. EventFilter is defined here (by workflow id, run, and/or event family).
All three traits are Send + Sync and object-safe (held as Arc<dyn ...> on the Engine), so AT/AD supply implementations without AE depending on their internals.
Doc comments name the implementing brief for each trait (SignalRouter -> AT-005/006, QueryService -> AT-007, EventPublisher -> AD/AT) so the seam is traceable.

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement signal routing into mailboxes, query dispatch to workflow processes, or the durable signal/query event recording — those are AT (delivery) and AD (recording).
SHALL NOT implement the event publisher / broadcast channel — that is AD/AT; this brief consumes its seam.
SHALL NOT add networking — WebSocket fan-out of the subscribe stream is AW; subscribe here returns an in-process stream.
verification: cargo test -p aion passes (with fake seams).
signal/query/subscribe perform only the registry lookup and the seam call; no AT/AD mechanism is implemented here (per the AE/AT/AD boundary).

checklist.text: Engine surfaces signal, query, and subscribe whose registry-lookup step lives here while their delivery mechanics delegate to AT (signal routing, query dispatch) and AD/AT (event publishing).

stories.text: As a workflow caller, I want to list and query workflows by type, status, and time so that I can see what is running and what has finished without loading full histories.

constraints.text: unsafe_code = deny. No unsafe in the crate.
No #[allow]/#[expect]/#[ignore] lint bypasses per CLAUDE.md; tests needing a runtime gate it at runtime, never #[ignore].
lib.rs/mod.rs are declarations and re-exports only.
aion depends only on aion-core, aion-store, aion-package, and beamr among workspace crates — no aion-server, no store backend crate, no networking crate. Structural; must hold.
Transport-agnostic: no HTTP, gRPC, or WebSocket dependency or code anywhere in the crate.
Only the runtime module imports beamr; every other module uses the RuntimeHandle (per D1).
All library errors are thiserror EngineError variants; no anyhow in this library crate. No .unwrap()/.expect() outside tests.
Mutex/RwLock poison handled explicitly and mapped to a typed EngineError; no .unwrap() on a lock guard.
WorkflowStatus is read from aion-core's event projection; the registry's cached status is never the source of truth (per aion-core CO7).
aion hardcodes no scheduler thread count, no timeout, and no retry default; configurable values come from the builder or are deferred to beamr's own default.
Module namespacing uses aion-package's transform unchanged; the engine never re-derives namespaced names (per D8).
Engine tests run against InMemoryStore; no test requires a database (per aion-core D8).

resolved_context.intention: Build the heart of the engine — the aion crate you embed. Given an EventStore, a set of loaded .aion workflow packages, and registered NIFs, EngineBuilder produces a live Engine that starts workflows, runs each as a BEAM process on an embedded beamr runtime, supervises them through a three-level tree, dispatches their activities as linked child processes, and cancels or reports on them by ID. Starting a workflow is spawning a process; cancelling it is killing that process, which through links kills its activity children too; an activity crash propagates an exit signal the trapping workflow process receives as a message. The crate is transport-agnostic — no HTTP/gRPC/WebSocket — keeping the embedded path free of network dependencies. This cluster owns lifecycle, process management, supervision, and module loading; it defers durability/replay (AD) and time/signals/queries/children/concurrency (AT) to their owners, surfacing their entry points on Engine and stating the seams explicitly.

provenance.quote: Build the heart of the engine — the aion crate you embed. Given an EventStore, a set of loaded .aion workflow packages, and registered NIFs, EngineBuilder produces a live Engine that starts workflows,
